attractions.name: Hayward Field
attractions.category: Track and Field Stadium / Landmark / University Facility
attractions.description: Hayward Field is a world-class track and field stadium located on the University of Oregon campus, widely regarded as the 'Theater for Track.' Rebuilt in 2020, it features a unique wood-ribbed roof, a nine-story landmark tower, and the interactive Hayward Hall museum. It is one of only three World Athletics Class 1 Facilities in the United States and serves as a premier venue for global competitions like the World Athletics Championships.

highlights: ['World Athletics Class 1 Facility rating, the highest global standard for track venues.', 'Nine-story landmark tower inspired by the Olympic Torch with an observation deck.', "Hayward Hall, a 4,000-square-foot interactive museum dedicated to Oregon's track and field history.", 'Innovative wood-ribbed roof structure designed to maximize acoustics and natural light.', 'Unobstructed sight lines with the first row of seats sitting just feet from the track level.', 'The largest permanent video screen for a track and field stadium in the U.S. (160 feet wide).']
attractions.tips: Visit Hayward Hall during its Tuesday-Friday operating hours for a free museum experience; check the official event schedule before visiting as the stadium bowl may be closed during private team practices; campus parking is limited, so consider using public transportation or nearby paid lots.

attractions.name: Autzen Stadium
attractions.description: Autzen Stadium is an iconic outdoor football stadium in Eugene, Oregon, serving as the home field for the University of Oregon Ducks. Opened in 1967 and significantly renovated in 2002, it is renowned for its intense atmosphere and is consistently ranked as one of the loudest venues in college football. The stadium features a FieldTurf playing surface and a massive "DuckVision" LED scoreboard. (Wikipedia, GoDucks.com)
attractions.hours: Ticket Office: 9:00 AM – 5:00 PM (Monday – Friday). Gameday: Gates typically open 3 hours prior to kickoff (GoDucks.com, GoDucks.com).
attractions.days_open: Monday - Friday (Ticket Office); Game days (Stadium events) (GoDucks.com)
attractions.admission: Prices vary by event and seating tier; 2025 single-game tickets range from approximately $22.50 to $1,701, with average prices around $117–$294 (eugenestadium.com). Season tickets for the 2025 season start at $692 (eugenestadium.com).
highlights: 'Consistently ranked among the top 10 loudest college football stadiums in the nation ([GoDucks.com).', 'Home of the "It never rains at Autzen Stadium" tradition and the "DuckVision" mega-screen (Wikipedia).', 'Features the Moshofsky Center, a massive indoor practice facility that hosts popular pre-game festivities (Eugene Cascades & Coast).', 'Famous game-day entrance featuring the Oregon Duck mascot leading the team on a motorcycle (Wikipedia).', 'Includes premium hospitality options like Sky Suites, the Charter Box, and the 10,000-square-foot OCCU Club (GoDucks.com).', 'Site of "The Pick," the legendary 1994 interception by Kenny Wheaton often credited with turning around the program\'s history (Wikipedia).']
attractions.tips: Use the Autzen Express Shuttle for easy transport or park at the Eugene Science Center lot nearby. Arrive early to experience the pre-game atmosphere in the Moshofsky Center, and walk the Autzen Footbridge from the UO campus for a scenic route. (GoDucks.com, Reddit)

attractions.name: Clear Lake Resort (McKenzie River Headwaters)
attractions.description: Clear Lake is a pristine mountain lake formed 3,000 years ago by volcanic activity that dammed the McKenzie River, creating a unique underwater ecosystem. It is world-renowned for its exceptionally clear, 43-degree water that preserves a prehistoric sunken forest visible up to 100 feet below the surface. The area serves as the headwaters of the McKenzie River and offers a year-round retreat for hiking, fishing, and non-motorized boating. Linn County Parks, Travel Oregon
attractions.address: 60700 Hwy 126 (Clear Lake Resort) or NF-775 (Forest Service Road) Linn County Parks, US Forest Service
attractions.hours: Lodge: 8:00 AM - 5:00 PM Daily; Restaurant: Mon-Thu 11:00 AM - 3:00 PM, Fri-Sun 8:00 AM - 5:00 PM; Day Use Area: 6:00 AM - 10:00 PM Linn County Parks, US Forest Service
attractions.admission: Day Use Parking: $9/day; Cabins and Yurts: Vary by season and type; Entry to the lake area is generally free Linn County Parks, US Forest Service
highlights: ['Sunken 3,000-year-old preserved forest visible through the water', 'Headwaters of the McKenzie River', '5-mile Clear Lake Loop Trail for hiking and mountain biking', 'Non-motorized boating with rowboat and kayak rentals available', 'Year-round resort accommodations including rustic cabins and yurts', 'Exceptional freshwater scuba diving amongst the sunken trees']
attractions.tips: Swimming is not recommended due to the near-freezing 43°F water temperatures year-round. All motorized watercraft are strictly prohibited on the lake to maintain its clarity. Reservations for cabins and yurts are highly recommended, especially during peak seasons. Linn County Parks, Eugene Cascades & Coast

attractions.name: Dorris Ranch
attractions.category: Museum, Park, and National Historic Site
attractions.description: Dorris Ranch is a historic 268-acre park and working commercial filbert orchard that serves as a living museum of Oregon's agricultural heritage. Established in 1892, it is recognized as the first commercial filbert orchard in the United States and is listed on the National Register of Historic Places. Visitors can explore miles of scenic trails, a historical living history village, and the 'Fast Forest,' which commemorates record-breaking track and field athletes. Willamalane Park and Recreation District

attractions.name: Downtown Eugene
attractions.description: Downtown Eugene is the social, economic, and cultural heart of the community, offering a vibrant urban atmosphere with a mix of local boutiques, art galleries, and diverse dining options. It is home to major cultural institutions such as the Hult Center for the Performing Arts and the historic McDonald Theatre, as well as the popular Eugene Saturday Market. The district is characterized by its extensive street art, historic landmarks like Kesey Square, and its central role as a hub for both the local transit system and community gatherings.

attractions.description: The Eugene Saturday Market is a vibrant weekly open-air craft market in Eugene, Oregon, known as the oldest continuously operating weekly open-air crafts festival in the United States. It features over 200 local artisans selling handmade goods, an international food court, and live musical performances in a festive community atmosphere. Eugene Saturday Market, Wikipedia

attractions.days_open: Saturdays (April through mid-November) Eugene Saturday Market FAQ
attractions.admission: Free Eugene Saturday Market FAQ, Tripadvisor
highlights: 'Over 200 local artisans selling exclusively handmade goods [Eugene, Cascades & Coast', 'International food court featuring diverse global cuisines Wikipedia', 'Live musical performances on the main stage and strolling entertainers Eugene Saturday Market', 'Oldest weekly open-air crafts festival in the United States, established in 1970 Wikipedia', 'Coincides with the Lane County Farmers Market located across the street Eugene Saturday Market']
attractions.tips: Arrive early (around 10 AM) for the best selection of artisan goods and produce. Bring both cash and cards, as some vendors may prefer cash for smaller transactions. Consider taking public transit to Eugene Station to avoid downtown parking challenges. Airial Travel, Tripadvisor

attractions.name: Falling Sky Brewing
attractions.description: Falling Sky Brewing is a flagship Eugene brewpub established in 2012 that specializes in handcrafting nearly everything it serves, including award-winning craft beers and seasonal food. The brewery serves as a community hub with a dog-friendly atmosphere, offering a collaborative experience of makers, brewers, and foodies in a welcoming downtown space. Falling Sky Brewing, Tripadvisor

attractions.tips: Visit on a Thursday to take advantage of the 'Burger & a Brew' special for a $1 pint with a burger purchase. Order a 4-beer flight to sample their diverse range of styles, from German lagers to tart sours. The brewpub is dog-friendly, making it a great spot to bring your pet. attractions.name: Heceta Head Lighthouse State Scenic Viewpoint
attractions.description: Heceta Head Lighthouse State Scenic Viewpoint features an iconic 56-foot lighthouse perched 206 feet above the Pacific Ocean, emitting the strongest light on the Oregon Coast visible for 21 miles. The park encompasses a historic lightkeeper's house now serving as a bed and breakfast, a sheltered beach at Cape Cove, and scenic hiking trails that connect to the Oregon Coast Trail. Visitors can explore the first floor of the lighthouse tower while enjoying premier vantage points for whale watching and sea bird observation (Oregon State Parks, Wikipedia).
attractions.hours: Park: Daylight to Sunset Daily. Lighthouse (1st Floor): 11:00 AM - 3:00 PM (March - October); 11:00 AM - 2:00 PM (November - February) (Oregon State Parks)
attractions.days_open: Daily (weather and staffing permitting) (Oregon State Parks)
attractions.admission: Free for lighthouse tours; Daily parking fee is $10 for Oregon residents and $12 for non-residents (Oregon State Parks, Oregon State Parks News)
highlights: ['Iconic 56-foot lighthouse tower first illuminated in 1894', 'Strongest light on the Oregon Coast, visible 21 miles out to sea', "Historic Assistant Lightkeeper's House, now a Bed & Breakfast", 'Premier location for whale watching and spotting sea lions and sea birds', 'Direct access to the 7-mile Heceta Lighthouse Trail network', 'Scenic views of the Pacific Ocean and the historic Cape Creek Bridge']
attractions.tips: Dress in layers as coastal weather is unpredictable, and wear sturdy walking shoes for the half-mile trail from the parking lot to the lighthouse. Arrive early during the summer months to ensure parking availability, as it is one of the most-visited lighthouses in the country (Oregon State Parks, LighthouseFriends).
attractions.nearby: Sea Lion Caves, Cape Perpetua Scenic Area, Hobbit Trail, Washburne Memorial State Park, and Historic Old Town Florence (Tripadvisor)

attractions.name: Historic Old Town Florence
attractions.description: Historic Old Town Florence is a charming 1900s fishing village nestled along the scenic Siuslaw River, offering a walkable blend of boutique shopping, waterfront dining, and historic architecture. Visitors can stroll the riverfront boardwalk, admire the iconic Siuslaw River Bridge, and explore a variety of art galleries and antique shops in a quaint coastal setting.
attractions.hours: Daily, typically 10:00 AM – 5:00 PM (Individual shop and restaurant hours vary; many open at 10:00 AM)
attractions.admission: Free (area access); Individual attractions like Siuslaw Pioneer Museum have small fees (~$5-$7)
highlights: ['Scenic riverfront boardwalk with views of the Siuslaw River Bridge and local wildlife like sea lions', 'A diverse collection of boutique shops, art galleries, and antique stores along Bay Street', 'Waterfront dining featuring fresh coastal seafood and local favorites like saltwater taffy and clam chowder', "Siuslaw Pioneer Museum, documenting the region's history from indigenous peoples to pioneer logging and fishing", 'Gazebo Park and riverfront green spaces perfect for picnics and people-watching', 'Annual community events including the Rhododendron Festival and monthly Second Saturday Art Walks']
attractions.tips: The area is highly walkable, so park once and explore on foot. Shops and restaurants may close earlier than in larger cities, so plan for an early dinner. Check the local events calendar for festivals like the 'Rhody Days' in May.
attractions.nearby: Siuslaw River Bridge, Oregon Dunes National Recreation Area, Heceta Head Lighthouse, Sea Lion Caves, Jessie M. Honeyman Memorial State Park

The Springfield location is the brewery's original flagship, offering a laid-back atmosphere with a full kitchen, extensive tap list, and a spacious pet-friendly patio. attractions.name: Hult Center for the Performing Arts
attractions.description: The Hult Center for the Performing Arts is a spacious and elegant regional venue located in the heart of downtown Eugene. Since its grand opening in 1982, it has been a cornerstone of the local arts scene, featuring stunning architectural and acoustical features like its soaring glass lobby and basket-weave ceiling in the Silva Concert Hall. (Hult Center)
attractions.hours: Ticket Office: Tuesday – Friday, Noon – 5 pm; Saturday, Noon – 3 pm. Administration: Tuesday – Friday, Noon – 4 pm. (Hult Center)

attractions.name: Iris Vineyards (Official Website)
attractions.description: Founded in 1992, Iris Vineyards is a historic producer and one of Oregon’s first 100 wineries, known for crafting elegant, dry-farmed wines. The winery focuses on Pinot Noir, Pinot Gris, and Chardonnay that reflect the unique cool-climate terroir of the Southern Willamette Valley (Official Website).
attractions.hours: Curbside pickup at the Cottage Grove winery is available by appointment; the previous Springfield wine bar location has transitioned to new ownership as of early 2026 (Official Website, Lookout Eugene-Springfield)
attractions.days_open: By appointment for winery services (Official Website)
attractions.admission: Tasting fees typically range from $15 to $20, often waived with a bottle purchase; curbside pickup is available by appointment (Tripadvisor, Official Website)
highlights: 'Specializes in cool-climate Pinot Noir, Pinot Gris, and Chardonnay ([Official Website)', 'Utilizes dry-farming techniques to ensure purity and depth in every bottle (Official Website)', 'Led by renowned winemaker Nicole Walsh, with over 29 vintages of experience (Official Website)', 'Features wines from the 1,000-acre Chalice Estate Vineyard in the Lorane Valley (Rose Hill B&B)', 'Consistently earns 90+ scores from top wine critics (Official Website)', 'Offers a variety of sparkling wines and wine-based cocktails (Oregon Wine Board)']
attractions.tips: Verify current tasting availability before visiting, as the downtown Springfield location closed in late 2025 and the estate vineyard primarily hosts private events and weddings (Lookout Eugene-Springfield, Wedding Spot).
attractions.nearby: King Estate Winery, Territorial Highway scenic drive, Dorris Ranch, and the Springfield History Museum (Tripadvisor)

attractions.description: The Lane County Farmers Market is Eugene's largest farmers market, featuring over 100 local farmers and food artisans. Operating primarily from the state-of-the-art Farmers Market Pavilion and Plaza, it provides a vibrant community space for purchasing fresh, in-season Oregon-grown produce and handcrafted food products. (Lane County Farmers Market) (Wikipedia)

attractions.name: McKenzie River Scenic Byway (Highway 126)
attractions.description: The McKenzie River Scenic Byway (Hwy 126) is an 80-mile route east of Eugene that follows the pristine McKenzie River through the Willamette National Forest. It showcases a diverse landscape of lush Douglas fir forests, dramatic waterfalls, and ancient lava fields as it climbs toward the Cascade Mountains. Visitors can explore iconic sites like Sahalie and Koosah Falls, the iridescent Tamolitch Blue Pool, and the volcanic summit of McKenzie Pass. U.S. Forest Service, Y Travel Blog
attractions.address: 57600 McKenzie Highway, McKenzie Bridge, OR 97413 U.S. Forest Service

attractions.days_open: Daily (General Access); Ranger Station: Monday-Friday U.S. Forest Service Offices
attractions.admission: Free for general access; $5 for National Forest Day Pass (valid at developed trailheads); $30 for Annual Northwest Forest Pass; $80 for Interagency Annual Pass U.S. Forest Service Passes
highlights: 'Sahalie and Koosah Falls: Stunning 100-foot and 70-foot waterfalls accessible via a short loop trail. [Rose Campau', 'Tamolitch Blue Pool: A famous iridescent blue pool where the river emerges from a lava tube. Y Travel Blog', 'Clear Lake: The headwaters of the McKenzie River, featuring preserved 3,000-year-old underwater trees. Y Travel Blog', 'McKenzie River Trail: A 26.5-mile trail popular for world-class mountain biking and hiking. Willamette Valley', 'Dee Wright Observatory: A stone structure at McKenzie Pass offering 360-degree views of Cascade peaks. U.S. Forest Service', 'Belknap Hot Springs: Relaxing mineral springs located along the river corridor. Travel with Wildly North']
attractions.tips: Get to popular trailheads like Tamolitch Blue Pool before 10:00 a.m. to avoid crowds and parking issues. Note that Highway 242 (McKenzie Pass) is typically closed from November to July due to snow. Y Travel Blog

attractions.name: Mount Pisgah Arboretum
attractions.description: Mount Pisgah Arboretum is a 209-acre nature education facility and botanical garden that features seven miles of all-weather trails winding through diverse native habitats, including riparian woods, meadows, and evergreen forests. The site serves as a "living tree museum" where visitors can explore the ecology of the Southern Willamette Valley through interactive learning and seasonal festivals. (Mount Pisgah Arboretum, Wikipedia)
attractions.hours: Arboretum: Dawn to dusk, every day of the year; Administrative Office: Mon – Fri, 9 AM – 3 PM (Mount Pisgah Arboretum)
attractions.days_open: Every day of the year (Mount Pisgah Arboretum)
attractions.admission: Free (No entry fee); $5 parking fee per vehicle (Mount Pisgah Arboretum)
highlights: 'Seven miles of all-weather trails with 23 bridges through diverse ecosystems ([Wikipedia)', 'Annual Wildflower & Music Festival in May and Mushroom Festival in October (Travel Lane County)', 'Extensive nature education programs for all ages and interactive interpretive exhibits (Mount Pisgah Arboretum)', 'Water garden featuring native species such as bullfrogs and turtles (Travel Lane County)', 'A popular venue for weddings and community events (Mount Pisgah Arboretum)']
attractions.tips: Groups larger than 10 should contact the office in advance and are encouraged to donate $1 per person. Bicycles can park for free, while vehicles must pay a $5 daily parking fee via onsite machines or the Passport Parking App. (Mount Pisgah Arboretum, Lane County Parks)

attractions.name: Oakridge Mountain Biking Trails
attractions.description: Oakridge is celebrated as the "Mountain Biking Capital of the Northwest," offering an expansive network of over 300 miles of world-class singletrack trails. Nestled in the Willamette National Forest, the area provides a diverse range of riding experiences, from flowy, scenic river-side paths to challenging, high-alpine descents with stunning Cascade Mountain views. (Travel Lane County)
attractions.address: 46375 Highway 58, Westfir, OR 97492 (Middle Fork Ranger Station)
attractions.hours: Trails: Open 24 hours daily (subject to seasonal snow); Middle Fork Ranger Station (Office): Monday - Friday, 10:00 AM - 4:30 PM (U.S. Forest Service)
attractions.admission: Free (parking at many trailheads requires a Northwest Forest Pass: $5 per day or $30 annually) (Discover NW)
highlights: 'Over 300 miles of world-class singletrack trails ([Travel Lane County)', 'Designated as an IMBA Gold-Level Ride Center (City of Oakridge)', "The Alpine Trail, known as the 'Crown Jewel' of Oakridge (Travel Lane County)", 'Professional shuttle services providing access to long downhill runs (Cog Wild)', 'Diamond View Park featuring a bicycle skills park for all ages (City of Oakridge)', 'Scenic routes through old-growth forests and along the Willamette River (Travel Oregon)']
attractions.tips: Check trail conditions with the Middle Fork Ranger District before riding, especially during the wet season. Practice trail etiquette by yielding to hikers and equestrians, and prepare for remote backcountry conditions by carrying sufficient water and tools. (Travel Lane County)
attractions.nearby: Salt Creek Falls, Willamette Pass Resort, Waldo Lake, 3 Legged Crane Pub and Brewhouse, Salmon Creek Falls (Travel Lane County)

attractions.name: Oregon Coastal Tour – Eugene to Florence to Yachats
attractions.description: The Oregon Coastal Tour from Eugene to Florence to Yachats is a full-day guided excursion showcasing the dramatic scenery of the Central Oregon Coast. Traveling in a Mercedes Sprinter van, visitors explore iconic landmarks including the Sea Lion Caves, Heceta Head Lighthouse, and the rugged Cape Perpetua Scenic Area.
attractions.address: 5th Street Public Market (Stop #3), Eugene, OR 97401 (Pickup location); or 91560 Highway 101, Florence, OR 97439 (Sea Lion Caves office)
attractions.hours: 8:30 AM – 5:30 PM (Pickup starting at 8:00 AM)
attractions.days_open: Wednesday, Friday (Public tours); Daily by appointment (Private tours)
attractions.admission: Public Tour: $140.00 per person. Private Tour: $1,500 (1 person), $750 (2), $500 (3), $375 (4), $300 (5), $250 (6+). Includes admission fees and water.
highlights: ["Sea Lion Caves: Descent into America's largest sea cave and year-round home of Steller sea lions.", "Heceta Head Lighthouse: Visit to the 205-foot-tall working lighthouse and historic keeper's home.", "Cape Perpetua Scenic Area: Exploration of natural wonders like Thor's Well, Devil's Churn, and Cook's Chasm.", 'Historic Old Town Florence: Shopping and sightseeing in a charming district on the Siuslaw River.', 'Coastal Towns: Discovery of the cliffside village of Yachats and its local galleries and boutiques.']
attractions.tips: Tours require a minimum of 4 people to operate; confirm status two weeks in advance. Bring extra layers for unpredictable coastal weather and comfortable shoes for the 1/2 mile hike to the lighthouse. Lunch is not included, but several dining options are provided in Yachats.
attractions.nearby: Fern Ridge Reservoir, Siuslaw National Forest, Oregon Dunes National Recreation Area, Sweet Creek Falls.

attractions.name: Oregon Dunes National Recreation Area
attractions.description: The Oregon Dunes National Recreation Area is one of the largest expanses of temperate coastal sand dunes in the world, stretching 40 miles along the central Oregon coast. This unique landscape features wind-sculpted sand dunes towering up to 500 feet above the ocean, interspersed with 'tree islands,' wetlands, and lush forests. It offers a diverse range of activities from high-adrenaline off-highway vehicle (OHV) riding to serene hiking and birdwatching in a striking desert-meets-ocean environment. Forest Service, Travel Oregon
attractions.hours: Recreation area is open daily (some areas have a midnight curfew for vehicles); Visitor Center is open Monday–Friday, 8:00 a.m. – 4:00 p.m. (Closed on federal holidays) Forest Service, Oregon's Adventure Coast
attractions.admission: $5 per vehicle per day (Recreation Pass required for most day-use sites; Free with valid Northwest Forest Pass or Interagency Pass) Forest Service
highlights: 'World-class off-highway vehicle (OHV) riding across expansive sand drifts.', 'Sandboarding and sand sledding on massive dunes near Florence.', 'Hiking trails through diverse ecosystems, including the Oregon Dunes Loop and Siltcoos Lake Trail.', 'Wildlife viewing opportunities for the Western Snowy Plover and Roosevelt Elk.', 'Scenic beachcombing and picnicking along 40 miles of pristine coastline.', "Photography of rare 'tree islands' and dramatic sunset views over the Pacific. [Forest Service"]
attractions.tips: Purchase your digital recreation pass online via Recreation.gov in advance, as cell service is spotty and many sites do not accept cash. Be aware of seasonal beach restrictions from March 15 to September 15 to protect the nesting Western Snowy Plover. Bring plenty of water and sun protection when hiking, as the open dunes offer no shade and the sand can be difficult to navigate. Forest Service, Eugene Cascades & Coast
attractions.nearby: Sweet Creek Falls, Dean Creek Elk Viewing Area, Heceta Head Lighthouse, Old Town Florence, Sand Master Park Travel Oregon

The area is best known as the longtime home of the Eugene Saturday Market and the Lane County Farmers Market, offering a space for local artisans, food vendors, and performers. attractions.name: Pre's Trail (City of Eugene)
attractions.description: Pre's Trail is a legendary 4-mile wood-chipped running and walking path located within Alton Baker Park. Inspired by Olympic runner Steve Prefontaine and modeled after European cross-country courses, the trail offers a soft, bark-covered surface designed to be easy on the legs. It winds through scenic riparian areas, grasslands, and woods, serving as a historic landmark and a pilgrimage site for running enthusiasts in "Tracktown USA." (Wikipedia, City of Eugene)
attractions.hours: Monday - Sunday: 6:00 AM - 11:00 PM (City of Eugene)
attractions.admission: Free (City of Eugene)
highlights: 'Signature soft wood-chip surface ideal for low-impact running ([Airial Travel)', 'Dedicated to local running legend Steve Prefontaine (Wikipedia)', 'Comprised of multiple loops (Red, Yellow, Green) totaling approximately 4 miles (City of Eugene)', 'Designated as a Eugene Historic Landmark in 2019 (Eugene, Cascades & Coast)', 'Seamlessly connects to the University of Oregon campus via the Frohnmayer Bike Bridge (City of Eugene)', 'Traverses diverse natural scenery including duck ponds and the Willamette River bank (Wikipedia)']
attractions.tips: The soft surface is excellent for recovery runs and training. It is recommended to carry your own water as fountains are not located directly on the trail loops. For the best photography and a peaceful experience, visit during the "golden hour" shortly after sunrise or before sunset. (Airial Travel)

attractions.name: Sahalie and Koosah Falls
attractions.description: Sahalie and Koosah Falls are two spectacular waterfalls on the McKenzie River, formed 3,000 years ago by basaltic lava flows that dammed Clear Lake. Sahalie Falls is a 100-foot plunge over a natural lava dam, while Koosah Falls drops approximately 70 feet into a deep pool. The two falls are connected by a scenic 2.6-mile loop trail that meanders through an ancient old-growth conifer forest. (Willamette National Forest, Eugene, Cascades & Coast)
attractions.hours: Monday - Sunday: Dawn to Dusk (Access is year-round but winter conditions may limit parking and trail visibility) (Willamette National Forest)
attractions.admission: Free (Eugene, Cascades & Coast)
highlights: ['100-foot Sahalie Falls plunge over a lava dam', '70-foot Koosah Falls dropping into a deep pool', '2.6-mile Waterfalls Loop Trail connecting both sites', 'Ancient old-growth conifer forest with Douglas fir and cedar', 'Connection to the McKenzie River National Recreation Trail', 'Geological interpretive panels explaining the 3,000-year-old lava flows']
attractions.tips: Visit in late spring (April-June) for the most powerful water flow; the Sahalie Falls observation deck is wheelchair accessible, but the full loop trail includes stairs; Koosah Falls parking is often less crowded than the main Sahalie Falls lot. (Eugene, Cascades & Coast)
attractions.nearby: Clear Lake, Blue Pool (Tamolitch Falls), Carmen Reservoir, Ice Cap Campground, McKenzie River National Recreation Trail (Willamette National Forest)

This picturesque corridor features over a dozen award-winning wineries, rolling pastoral hills, and historic covered bridges. Visitors can enjoy intimate tasting experiences at family-owned vineyards or grand estates, paired with the region's renowned Pinot Noir and Pinot Gris. Originally an agricultural area, it has transformed into a premier fermentation district filled with renowned breweries, wineries, and distilleries, while remaining a stronghold for community activism and alternative lifestyles.

attractions.name: Willamette Pass Resort
attractions.description: Willamette Pass Resort is a premier ski area in the Cascade Range of Oregon, featuring 555 acres of skiable terrain and 29 trails. It is home to Oregon's fastest six-person chairlift and is known for having one of the steepest runs in the world (Wikipedia, Willamette Pass).

attractions.days_open: Typically open Wednesday through Sunday during the winter season, with daily operations during peak holiday periods (e.g., Dec 26 - Jan 4) (Willamette Pass, Willamette Pass Updates).
attractions.admission: Lift tickets as low as $19 when booked in advance; Kids 12 and under ski FREE with Power Kids Pass; Super Seniors (75+) ski FREE; XC skiing tickets $15 at the ticket window. Daily prices vary by age and date (Willamette Pass, OnTheSnow).

attractions.description: The Oregon Wine L.A.B. (Local Artisan Brands) is Eugene's premier urban winery and tasting lounge, serving as the production home for William Rose Wines. It offers a modern, relaxed atmosphere where guests can sample wines from small, local producers who lack their own tasting rooms. The venue features a spacious, covered outdoor patio and a cozy indoor lounge, often hosting food carts and community events. Oregon Wine L.A.B., Eugene Cascades & Coast
attractions.hours: Tuesday – Saturday: 12:00 PM – 9:00 PM; Sunday: 12:00 PM – 5:00 PM; Monday: Closed Oregon Wine L.A.B.
attractions.admission: Free entry; Tasting flights typically range from $12–$15 Yelp, Tripadvisor
highlights: 'Home to William Rose Wines, produced on-site from Oregon-grown grapes [Oregon Wine L.A.B.', 'Showcases "Local Artisan Brands" (L.A.B.) from small, independent wineries across the region Oregon Wine L.A.B.', 'Features a large, dog-friendly outdoor patio and cozy indoor "cafe vibes" Oregon Wine L.A.B.', 'On-site food available from the Uumami Mediterranean food cart Oregon Wine L.A.B.', 'Hosts regular events including trivia nights, live music, and "Bubbles & Beats" Tripadvisor', 'Offers a "OWL’s Nest" private function room for events and parties Oregon Wine L.A.B.']
attractions.tips: Reservations are highly encouraged to ensure table availability. Minors are welcome in the tasting room and patio until 9:00 PM. Check their online calendar for the food cart schedule and special events like trivia. Oregon Wine L.A.B.

attractions.name: Wandering Goat Coffee Co. (Wandering Goat Coffee)
attractions.description: Wandering Goat Coffee Co. is a locally owned coffee roaster and cafe located in the heart of Eugene's Whiteaker neighborhood. They specialize in light-to-medium roasts and are dedicated to sourcing 100% certified organic, shade-grown coffees produced using sustainable methods. The cafe offers a funky, offbeat vibe with a full-service menu including organic coffee, vegan food, and occasional live music events (Wandering Goat Coffee, Eugene Weekly).
attractions.hours: Monday: 7:00 AM - 3:00 PM, Tuesday: 7:00 AM - 3:00 PM, Wednesday: 7:00 AM - 3:00 PM, Thursday: 7:00 AM - 3:00 PM, Friday: 7:00 AM - 3:00 PM, Saturday: 7:00 AM - 3:00 PM, Sunday: 7:00 AM - 3:00 PM (Wandering Goat Coffee)
attractions.admission: Free (Tripadvisor)
highlights: '100% certified organic and shade-grown coffee beans roasted on-site ([Wacaco)', 'Unique signature drinks like the "Cafe Borgia," a mocha infused with orange zest (Yelp)', '100% vegan food menu featuring items like biscuits and gravy and house-made pastries (Wandering Goat Coffee)', 'Vibrant atmosphere that frequently hosts live music ranging from folk to punk rock (Eugene Weekly)', 'Sustainability-focused business practices, including Oregon Tilth certification (Wandering Goat Coffee)', 'Indoor and outdoor seating options in the historic Whiteaker district (Wandering Goat Coffee)']
attractions.tips: Try the Cafe Borgia for a unique citrus-chocolate coffee experience. If the cafe's small parking lot is full, there is additional street parking available nearby. It is a great spot for journaling or meeting friends, though be aware that it can get busy during live music events (Yelp).

attractions.name: Whiteaker Block Party (WBP)
attractions.description: The Whiteaker Block Party is a vibrant, community-driven street festival that celebrates the eclectic and bohemian spirit of Eugene's historic Whiteaker neighborhood. The event features multiple stages with live local music, street performers, art installations, food vendors, and a showcase of the area's famous craft breweries. Eugene Weekly, South River Structures
attractions.address: 420 Blair Blvd (primary event hub/organizer address) and multiple blocks throughout the Whiteaker neighborhood, including 3rd Ave & Blair Blvd
attractions.hours: Saturday: 12:00 PM – 10:00 PM Facebook, Instagram
attractions.admission: Free (some "Unofficial" venues/after-parties may have cover charges ranging from $5 to $17) Eugene Weekly, KVAL
attractions.phone: 541-393-6820 (Twenty After Four Wellness Center - Organizing Hub) Leafly
highlights: 'Dozens of local bands and musical performances across multiple stages [Eugene Weekly', 'Diverse selection of local food carts and street vendors KVAL', 'Live art installations, crafts, and interactive community activities Instagram', "Showcase of neighborhood staples like Sam Bond's Garage and Ninkasi Brewery Eugene, Cascades & Coast", 'Unique street performances including fire spinners and aerialists Facebook', 'Family-friendly Kid Zone and community history displays Eugene Weekly']
attractions.tips: Arrive early to explore all nine stages and avoid the largest crowds; the event is bike-friendly and often provides a bike valet. Wear comfortable walking shoes as the party spans multiple city blocks, and be sure to check the individual venue schedules for 'Unofficial' after-parties. Eugene Weekly, KVAL

attractions.name: Amazon Park
attractions.description: Amazon Park is a vibrant, active community space spanning nearly 100 acres in the heart of south Eugene. It offers a diverse range of recreational opportunities, including a 5.5-mile bark running trail, Eugene's only outdoor pool, and a groundbreaking fully-inclusive playground. Eugene, OR Website, Kupi.com
attractions.hours: Park: Daily, daylight hours; Amazon Community Center: Monday-Friday, 9:00 AM – 5:00 PM; Amazon Pool: Seasonal (late May to September) with varying swim schedules. Eugene, OR Website, Kupi.com
attractions.admission: Park Admission: Free; Amazon Pool: Single visit $7 for adults (18+), $6 for youth (3-17) and seniors (60+), and $20 for families. Eugene, OR Website
highlights: '5.5-mile North and South Amazon Running Trail System (bark jogging path) [Eugene, OR Website', "Eugene's first fully-inclusive playground for all ages and mobility Eugene, OR Website", "Amazon Pool, the city's only outdoor public pool facility Eugene, OR Website", '3-acre dog park for all sizes and a separate 0.5-acre area for small dogs Eugene, OR Website', 'Extensive sports facilities including tennis courts, soccer fields, and a skate park Eugene, OR Website', 'Amazon Community Center offering enrichment classes and a community garden Eugene, OR Website']
attractions.tips: The pool is a major local attraction in the summer; arrive early as lines can be long. Runners should utilize the 5.5-mile bark trail for a low-impact surface compared to city sidewalks. Tripadvisor, Eugene, OR Website
attractions.nearby: Spencer Butte Park, University of Oregon campus, Hilyard Community Center. Wikipedia, Great Runs

attractions.name: Cascades Raptor Center Cascades Raptor Center
attractions.category: Nature Center & Wildlife Hospital Cascades Raptor Center
attractions.description: Cascades Raptor Center is a premier nature center and wildlife hospital in Eugene, Oregon, dedicated to the rehabilitation of birds of prey and public education. The facility is home to over 30 resident education raptors representing 30 native species, making it one of the largest collections of its kind in the Pacific Northwest. Cascades Raptor Center, SimpleTix
attractions.address: 32275 Fox Hollow Rd. Cascades Raptor Center
attractions.hours: Tuesday – Sunday: 10:00 AM – 5:00 PM. Closed Mondays (except Memorial Day), Thanksgiving Day, Christmas Eve, and Christmas Day. Cascades Raptor Center
attractions.days_open: Tuesday, Wednesday, Thursday, Friday, Saturday, Sunday Cascades Raptor Center
attractions.admission: Adults: $16, Seniors (62+): $14, Students (13+): $14, Military (Veterans and Active Duty): $14, Children (4-12): $10, Children under 4: Free. Free for Members, DSPs, and EBT cardholders (up to 4 people) through Museums for All. Cascades Raptor Center, Head Start of Lane County
highlights: 'Home to nearly 40 resident birds of prey including owls, eagles, and falcons [SimpleTix', 'One of the largest native raptor collections in the Pacific Northwest Cascades Raptor Center', 'The only raptor-specific wildlife clinic in Oregon, treating hundreds of patients annually Cascades Raptor Center', 'Wooded hillside setting with self-guided tours through outdoor aviaries Tripadvisor', 'Specializes in species like turkey vultures, harriers, and ospreys SimpleTix', 'Educational programs and seasonal raptor meet-and-greets Cascades Raptor Center']
attractions.tips: The center is entirely outdoors, so dress for the weather and wear sturdy walking shoes for gravel paths. No dogs are allowed on site, including in vehicles. Parking is limited; use the overflow lot across the street on Christensen Road if needed. Cascades Raptor Center, SimpleTix

attractions.name: Eugene Civic Alliance (Civic Park)
attractions.description: Eugene Civic Alliance is a nonprofit corporation that built and operates Civic Park, a premier sports and recreation destination on the historic Civic Stadium property. The facility serves as a hub for youth and adult sports, featuring a 40,000-square-foot fieldhouse and a FIFA regulation-size synthetic turf field to promote community health and fitness. Eugene Civic Alliance, Travel Oregon
attractions.hours: Office: 8:30 AM - 4:30 PM (Monday - Friday); Facility: Open daily for scheduled events and programs Kidsports, Play Civic Park
attractions.admission: Free for public park use; varies for specific leagues and events Eugene Civic Alliance, Play Civic Park
highlights: ['Kidsports Fieldhouse with 4 hardwood multi-purpose courts and 2 outdoor courts', 'FIFA regulation-size synthetic turf field (Market of Choice Field)', '2,500-seat grandstand for professional and amateur sporting events', 'Home to Lane United Football Club and Bushnell University soccer teams', 'Community event spaces and meeting rooms available for rental', "Pocket park and children's play area within the facility"]
attractions.tips: Free parking is available in the North Parking Lot off 20th Avenue and Amazon Parkway during operating hours. Visitors should check the event calendar or rental schedule as the turf field and courts are often reserved for specific leagues and programs. Play Civic Park

attractions.name: Hendricks Park Rhododendron Garden
attractions.description: Hendricks Park is Eugene's oldest city park, featuring 80 acres of diverse natural beauty including a world-renowned rhododendron garden and a native plant garden. Visitors can explore a 58-acre urban forest with 200-year-old Douglas firs and access the northern terminus of the 12-mile Ridgeline Trail (Eugene, OR Official Website, Friends of Hendricks Park).
attractions.address: Summit Ave. and Skyline Blvd., Eugene, OR 97403
attractions.hours: 6:00 a.m. - 11:00 p.m. daily (Friends of Hendricks Park, Oregon Hikers)
attractions.admission: Free (Airial Travel, Virtual Tourist)
highlights: '12-acre Rhododendron Garden with over 6,000 varieties of plants ([Eugene, OR Official Website)', 'Ancient Douglas fir trees, some over 200 years old (Eugene, OR Official Website)', 'Francis M. Wilkins Shelter and Moon Terrace stone deck (Eugene, OR Official Website)', 'Native Plant Garden featuring ferns and local wildflowers (Eugene, OR Official Website)', 'Steve Prefontaine Memorial near the park entrance (Airial Travel)', 'Scenic hiking and running trails connecting to the Ridgeline Trail system (Eugene, OR Official Website)']
attractions.tips: Visit from late April to early June to see peak rhododendron blooms. Dogs are allowed in the forest area on-leash but are strictly prohibited within the Rhododendron and Native Plant Gardens to protect the flora. Parking is available in the upper lot at 1800 Skyline Blvd or near the main entrance (Eugene, OR Official Website, Airial Travel).
attractions.nearby: University of Oregon, Jordan Schnitzer Museum of Art, Hayward Field, Alton Baker Park, Skinner Butte Park (TripAdvisor, Reddit)

attractions.name: Prefontaine Memorial (Pre's Rock)
attractions.description: Prefontaine Memorial Park is a 1.3-acre site located at the hairpin turn on Skyline Boulevard where legendary American distance runner Steve Prefontaine died in 1975. The site features a black granite memorial stone and has become a global pilgrimage destination for athletes who leave medals, race bibs, and shoes in tribute. City of Eugene, Atlas Obscura
attractions.hours: Daily: 6:00 AM - 11:00 PM City of Eugene
attractions.admission: Free City of Eugene
highlights: ['Site of the 1975 fatal crash of Olympic runner Steve Prefontaine', 'Black granite memorial slab with an engraved picture and tribute poem', 'Tradition of visitors leaving personal running memorabilia like medals and shoes', 'Maintained as a 1.3-acre park by the City of Eugene since 1997', 'Located on a scenic, winding road overlooking the city and near Hendricks Park']
attractions.tips: Parking is extremely limited and the road is narrow and winding; pedestrians should use caution and stay behind the safety rail. Visiting on foot from Hayward Field (approx. 1 mile away) is a popular tradition for runners. Eugene, Cascades & Coast
attractions.nearby: Hendricks Park, Hayward Field, Pre's Trail (Alton Baker Park), University of Oregon TripAdvisor

attractions.name: Spencer Butte Park
attractions.description: Spencer Butte Park is a regional landmark and the highest point along Eugene's 2,100-acre Ridgeline Park system. Standing at 2,058 feet, its treeless summit provides stunning 360-degree views of the Willamette Valley, Cascade Mountains, and Coast Range. City of Eugene, Spencer Butte Park
attractions.address: 85397 Willamette St, Eugene, OR 97405 City of Eugene
attractions.hours: Daily, 6:00 a.m. – 11:00 p.m. City of Eugene/KMTR, Trip.com
attractions.admission: Free City of Eugene, Hikespeak
highlights: '2,058-foot summit with panoramic 360-degree views [City of Eugene', 'Main Summit Trail featuring artful dry-stone masonry stairways City of Eugene', 'Part of the extensive 12-mile Ridgeline Trail System Spencer Butte Park', 'Diverse ecosystem including old-growth Douglas fir forests and rare wildflowers City of Eugene', 'Volcanic bedrock core of basaltic andesite formed millions of years ago Spencer Butte Park']
attractions.tips: Wear sturdy hiking shoes and bring plenty of water as the trails can be steep. Stay on designated paths to protect the sensitive summit habitat and rare plants. Note that parking is free but fills up quickly on weekends, and hikers are advised not to leave valuables in their vehicles. City of Eugene, Spencer Butte Park

attractions.name: The Bier Stein Bottleshop & Pub
attractions.description: The Bier Stein is a connoisseur beer store and beer hall located in Eugene, OR, featuring over 1,000 different bottles of beer from around the world and 25+ evolving taps. Originally opened in 2005, it offers a world-class beer experience paired with a menu of house specialties, sandwiches, and traditional German-style pub food.

attractions.name: King Estate Winery
attractions.description: Founded in 1991, King Estate is a premier family-owned winery located on a 1,033-acre estate southwest of Eugene, Oregon. It is recognized as the largest Biodynamic® certified vineyard in North America and is a benchmark producer of Pinot Gris. The estate features a unique culinary ecosystem including vineyards, orchards, and gardens that support its on-site gourmet restaurant King Estate, Wikipedia
